title: Local presence/transport daemon (eliminate the per-session holder)
summary: Design foundation merged (the local-exchange model, ADRs 0014-0024 in docs/design/). Building the per-user local exchange (daemon) for SQLite + Postgres, the Copilot plugin (sessionEnd hook + skill), seamless upgrade, and a validation-loop hardening wave (chaos + Entra-PG multi-host + AKS scale), so idle long-lived sessions stay wakeable and stations stop going stale.

nodes.title: Design foundation: daemon architecture, liveness model, protocol, upgrade
nodes.summary: Make the daemon architecture explicit and validated: DECISIONS ADR(s), DESIGN.md station->daemon update, PRODUCT-THESIS.md no-server->local-daemon update, the IPC/attendance protocol, the liveness model (hook healthy-path + a generic, typed --watch-pid pid-watch backstop, no idle-TTL teardown) plus minimal stale-attendance (last-confirmed + occupied_stale + takeover, no teardown), the lease-epoch fencing token (epoch-guarded heartbeat/release; self-demote on a 0-row heartbeat), the daemon singleton identity (user SID + config root + protocol-major), durable-buffer reuse of 0011/0013, and the epoch-based upgrade/handoff design, the server-side epoch fence on delivery emission + ordered handoff, the seen-dedup redesign for a long-lived daemon, the daemon lifecycle contract + Status surface, the daemon-scoped capability/version-handshake IPC, daemon-native session RPCs (Register/Re-register/DeregisterSession), the takeover state algebra, and the verb/docs cutover. Resolves the open implementation-design questions.

nodes.title: Design accepted
nodes.summary: Builder validates the daemon architecture, liveness model, protocol, and upgrade design before any implementation proceeds. Spar + council critique resolved.

nodes.title: Daemon core + one-shot verbs (SQLite)
nodes.summary: Auto-spawned per-user single-instance daemon (singleton keyed by user SID + config root + protocol-major) owning the SQLite poll loop, durable buffer (reuse 0011/0013 deliveries), daemon-scoped IPC (attach/wait/ack/detach/send/reply/status; explicit-only membership; frames carry the lease epoch), in-memory explicit-only membership + NeedsAttach re-attach, daemon-written epoch-guarded lease heartbeat (self-demote on a 0-row heartbeat), server-side epoch-gated delivery emission (mark_delivered_if_current_owner; no Message frame unless the daemon owns the epoch), the redesigned bounded/durable dedup (the seen invariant no longer relies on holder restart), and a generic typed --watch-pid pid-watch backstop (v1 floor: loader anchor + start-time; harness-agnostic). Refactor attach/detach into one-shot register/deregister and wait into a daemon client, so a session runs zero persistent telex processes. Includes the minimal upgrade floor (versioned shim + daemon stop --drain + next-call respawn + legacy-holder/non-epoch-lease cutover rule) and the daemon lifecycle contract (OS spawn-lock, connect-or-spawn, readiness ACK, wait reconnect-on-EOF grace, exit codes, Status surface); acceptance includes the four lifecycle tests (concurrent first-use, crash-during-wait, competing daemons, handoff duplicates). Implements the normative contract docs/design/daemon.md; acceptance = daemon.md sec.17 gating tests + per-backend conformance. Design for testability: keep epoch / delivery-records / membership / lease state inspectable (the Status surface is the seam) so the validation harness can assert invariants. Acceptance requires a real-process integration tier (real-binary daemon + one-shot verbs over the real IPC endpoint) for the multi-process / OS-primitive / real-kill subset of sec.17 (tests 1, 2, 14, and real killed-process crash recovery for 3/4/13/17), complementing the in-process TestDaemon suite; exotic cross-principal subcases may be platform-gated with a tracking ref. With the Copilot plugin this is the first operator-unblocking slice: holder/waiter race + orphans + turn-loop starvation gone on SQLite.

nodes.title: Fencing proof (epoch-guarded emission + ordered handoff)
nodes.summary: Executable proof that delivery emission is epoch-gated server-side and that graceful handoff/crash cannot double-deliver or flip ownership, on SQLite first. Proves daemon.md sec.11.3-11.4 (server-side delivery fence + ordered handoff) via the sec.17 gating tests. Distinct gate that postgres-parity, copilot-plugin, and seamless-upgrade depend on; downstream branches must not proceed until it passes.

nodes.title: Postgres parity
nodes.summary: One LISTEN/NOTIFY connection in the daemon; daemon heartbeats local attendees' backend leases; per-machine presence with the backend lease as cross-machine source of truth. The daemon-respawn reclaim race is handled by epoch fencing; this node must prove epoch behavior under competing daemons/machines before it lands. Sketch; detailed at promotion.

nodes.title: Copilot plugin: sessionEnd hook + skill
nodes.summary: Merged in PR #44: plugin package, sessionEnd hook mapped to daemon SessionEnd, single-source skill plumbing, and the `copilot.rs` harness boundary. The merged skill is waiter-oriented (classic `telex wait` / re-arm / out-dir path) and becomes rework input to #53: Copilot delivery pivots to bind -> load bridge -> receive pushed turns -> disposition, while `telex wait` remains the generic pull primitive for non-Copilot/non-extension harnesses.

nodes.title: Liveness & visibility hardening (deaf-evidence, terminal-exit-status, foreign-lease view)
nodes.summary: Generic hardening after the #53 bridge pivot: terminal status on EVERY `telex wait` exit for non-Copilot/pull-mode users; an --all-sessions / foreign-lease view so a session-scoped status no longer hides a ghost lease; and any bridge health/orphan-extension visibility not carried directly by #53. The Copilot-specific deaf-waiter unblock moves to push-delivery, so this is useful hardening but no longer a closure gate by itself.

nodes.title: Push delivery for Copilot bridge sessions
nodes.summary: Implement the #53 design of record: Copilot delivery via a load-on-bind in-session bridge extension using `joinSession()` + local authenticated `/send` + `session.send({prompt, mode:"enqueue"})`, with a generic daemon on-deliver exec as the Copilot-agnostic trigger. `telex attach --copilot-bridge` materializes the session-scoped extension and registers the on-deliver handler; `extensions_reload` loads it live; `telex detach` unloads it on the last binding. Copilot no longer arms, re-arms, or reasons about `telex wait`; the merged #41 skill content is reworked to bind -> load bridge -> receive pushed turns -> disposition. `telex wait` remains for generic pull users. No --ui-server requirement for the common case.

nodes.title: Seamless upgrade (#6 / PR #56)
nodes.summary: Practical seamless-upgrade floor that closes #6 under the narrowed bar: versioned install + stable launcher, local upgrade/switch (upgrade --from is acceptable), current/previous manifests, conservative known-safe rollback (not a general downgrade guarantee), conservative GC preserving current/previous/active versions, incompatible/too-old binary fail-closed diagnostics, status/version mixed-version observability, install script migration, and current Copilot bridge version/GC needs. GitHub release discovery, full rollback/downgrade framework, Postgres live successor handoff beyond safe drain/release, stronger GC scanning, extra Unix/fsync hardening, and destructive direct-pre-epoch Postgres migrations are future hardening.

nodes.title: Validation harness + Tier 1/2 loop (chaos + Entra-PG multi-host)
nodes.summary: Derive the machine-checkable invariant suite from the merged design (no-dup / no-loss / single-writer / non-destructive-reaping / idle-wakeable / upgrade-transparent) and build a portable harness + synthetic session driver + fault injector. Run the Tier 1 (SQLite chaos: many sessions, daemon kill/restart mid-delivery, competing daemons, handoff under load) and Tier 2 (real Entra-PG, multi-host via containers: cross-machine lease/reclaim/fence under daemon deaths/partitions) validation loop; file/repair/rerun. Invariants + observability hooks are derived against the implemented reality (Status surface is the seam); missing observability is a bounded repair issue back to daemon-core.

nodes.title: AKS large-network harness spike
nodes.summary: Prove the approach for a large network of telex hosts on AKS: N daemon pods (one local exchange per pod = a host) + M synthetic sessions driving attach/wait/ack/send against the shared Entra PG, with fault injection. Validate feasibility, orchestration, cost, and the metrics/oracle pipeline. Runs in parallel with validation-harness; its output shapes scale-rig-and-loop.

nodes.title: AKS scale rig + scale/stress loop (last)
nodes.summary: Build the full AKS large-network scale rig (reusing the synthetic driver from validation-harness, informed by aks-scale-spike) and run the scale/stress validation loop against the Entra PG: large attendee counts, churn, contention, throughput, and partition/upgrade-under-load. Measures and closes the carried perf issues (#26 per-tick scan index, #27 mark_delivered cap, #24 registry GC). Produces the scale evidence for the hardening gate.

nodes.title: Hardening gate (validation report)
nodes.summary: Demonstration gate: builder consumes the full validation report (Tier 1/2 chaos + Entra-PG multi-host + AKS scale) - scenario exercised, evidence, gaps found, repairs + reruns, remaining accepted risk - and judges whether the system has earned rollout confidence. Blocks closure-gate.

nodes.title: End-to-end unblock + closure
nodes.summary: Builder validates the full system: give a real session the new plugin + new telex and confirm it is unblocked end-to-end. Retire superseded mechanisms (pid-watch-as-holder, #3 relay, the re-arm dance) and update SKILL.md.

checkpoints.title: Design foundation aligned
checkpoints.summary: Daemon architecture, liveness model, protocol, durable-buffer reuse, and upgrade design are explicit and builder-validated.

checkpoints.title: Daemon core usable + fencing proven (SQLite)
checkpoints.summary: Daemon core on SQLite with server-side epoch-fenced delivery proven (fencing-proof); a session attends and receives with zero per-session telex processes. The SQLite operator-unblock slice is in reach, needing the plugin.

checkpoints.title: Both backends + Copilot plugin landed
checkpoints.summary: Postgres parity and the Copilot plugin (explicit Detach hook + single-source skill) landed; with the plugin on SQLite the first operator-unblock slice is shippable.

checkpoints.title: Coordination hardening (bridge push + generic liveness visibility)
checkpoints.summary: The #45 dogfooding friction is addressed by the #53 Copilot bridge push path (no agent-managed waiter/re-arm loop for Copilot) plus remaining generic liveness/visibility hardening for non-Copilot/pull-mode users. The former cc-stream-wake node is folded into #53's bridge/attention model for Copilot.

checkpoints.title: Full deliverable complete
checkpoints.summary: The full seamless upgrade platform (#6) landed; both backends, the plugin, and upgrade are all in, pending closure validation.

checkpoints.title: System hardened (validation loop)
checkpoints.summary: The validation-loop wave earned rollout confidence: Tier 1/2 chaos + Entra-PG multi-host + AKS scale exercised, gaps repaired and reruns passed, perf issues #26/#27/#24 closed, remaining risk explicitly accepted.

checkpoints.title: Closure ready
checkpoints.summary: End-to-end unblock validated; superseded mechanisms and docs retired/updated.
